title: Religious and racially-motivated hate crime: where to report it and find victim support
body: "Use this guidance to find support if you have been a victim of racially or religiously-motivated hate crime. You can also report a hate crime you have witnessed.  Call 999 if you’re reporting a crime that’s in progress or if someone is in immediate danger.  Hate Crime  True Vision provide advice on hate crime, how to report it, and where to get support.  Stop Hate record hate incidents and provide third party support for all victims of hate crime.  GOV.UK also provides advice on how to report hate crime.  Citizens Advice provides further information and guidance on  reporting a hate crime or hate incident to the police.  Racially-motivated hate crime  On Your Side record and report hate incidents directed against East or Southeast Asian individuals and provide support for anybody in the UK who identifies as part of an East or Southeast Asian community.  Rakkha record and report hate incidents directed against Sikh and South Asian individuals, and provide support for Sikh and South Asian victims of hate.  Religiously-motivated hate crime  Community Security Trust (CST) record and report antisemitic hate incidents and provide support for victims of antisemitism.  Tell MAMA record and report anti-Muslim (Islamophobic) hate incidents and provide support for victims of anti-Muslim hate. "
